chapter_title.en: Chapter on What Has Been Reported Regarding a Man Who Strikes Another Man, Causing Continuous Urination

text: 5315 - وَ رَوَى غِيَاثُ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "أَنَّ عَلِيّاً عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَضَى فِي رَجُلٍ ضُرِبَ حَتَّى سَلِسَ بَوْلُهُ بِالدِّيَةِ اَلْكَامِلَةِ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5315 - It was narrated by Ghiyath ibn Ibrahim from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) that:
"Indeed, Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) ruled concerning a man who was struck until he suffered from continuous urination (incontinence) that he is entitled to the full blood money (diyyah)."
